note: naked (HTML source only) vs harness (rendered page + axe + a11y tree + contrast). Renderable pages only — NOT the source-code bench tasks. Separate from leaderboard.json.
methodology: naked is given ONLY the page HTML; harness opens the rendered page. On the render-dependent suite the contrast violations live in EXTERNAL CSS — computed/rendered values a source-reading model cannot determine (cascade, overrides, background blending), so naked is blind to them while the harness measures them with check_contrast. The inline-CSS baseline keeps colors in the HTML, so naked can read them and the two modes roughly tie — which is exactly why the harness edge shows up only on render-dependent pages. Pages are authored eval fixtures with complete gold annotations + accessible decoys (so over-reporting is penalised); they are NOT scraped single-PR tasks. Figures are pooled (micro-average) over 3 runs.
headline: 3-run average. Combined (18 pages, 168 findings): harness recall 84.5% / precision 89.3% vs naked 69.0% / 79.5%. Render-dependent subset (8 pages, 72 findings): harness 91.7% / 91.7% vs naked 40.3% / 53.7%.
